assertions.text: status_card_present: Response contains the formatted box status card
assertions.evidence: Response uses markdown headers, a table, and bullet lists. No ASCII box card with a SPREAD MONITOR header is present.

assertions.text: zone_classified: Response clearly states one of the five zones
assertions.evidence: Response describes the spread as 'comfortably out of the money' and 'NOT be particularly worried' but never assigns one of the five zone labels (SAFE, WATCH, WARNING, DANGER, ACT NOW).

assertions.text: flags_earnings_risk: Response mentions the Citigroup earnings event (~April 14 2026) as a risk factor for this position
assertions.evidence: 'Q1 2026 earnings are April 14, 2026 — before market open — confirmed. This falls WITHIN your expiration window.' Earnings risk section is present and clearly highlighted.

assertions.text: shows_buffer_pct: Response shows the % buffer between current stock price and the $99 short strike
assertions.evidence: 'the stock needs to fall ~9.6% to reach your short strike ($99)' — percentage buffer is stated explicitly.

assertions.text: shows_pnl: Response shows current P&L and cost to close the spread
assertions.evidence: Response states the credit received ($1.14) and max loss ($1,290/contract implicitly from spread width) but does not show current P&L (gain or loss to date) or the current cost to close the spread.

assertions.text: actionable_guidance: Response gives a concrete suggestion — either hold with awareness of earnings or close/roll before April 14
assertions.evidence: 'No action today. Set a mental alert if C approaches $101–$102 post-earnings. If it trades there with 2 weeks left, consider closing early to lock in partial profit.' Concrete earnings-aware guidance is present.
